Research output: Local inference (Ollama) is preferred for heartbeat/hygiene tasks because it eliminates per-call API costs and network latency, enabling high-frequency polling without budget pressure or rate-limit exposure. Keeping routine, low-stakes tasks local also reduces attack surface and dependency on external availability, while reserving cloud inference for complex reasoning tasks that genuinely benefit from frontier models.
